name: Tennis
description: Grand Slams, legendary players, and tennis history

questions.q: How many Grand Slam tournaments make up the 'tennis calendar year'?

source.notes: Australian Open, French Open (Roland Garros), Wimbledon, US Open.

questions.q: In what year was the first Wimbledon Championship held?

source.notes: Oldest tennis tournament in the world. Held in Wimbledon, London.

questions.q: Which company has supplied the tennis balls at Wimbledon continuously since 1902?

source.notes: Longest-running sporting sponsorship in history.

questions.q: What surface is the Wimbledon Championships played on?

source.notes: Only grass-court Grand Slam. Traditional 'lawn tennis' surface.

questions.q: Which Grand Slam is played on clay courts at Roland Garros?

source.notes: Red clay (crushed brick). Rafael Nadal has won 14 French Open titles.

questions.q: Which Swiss player won 20 Grand Slam singles titles during his career?

source.notes: Career Grand Slam winner. Retired September 2022.

questions.q: Who became the youngest ever Wimbledon men's singles champion at age 17 in 1985?

source.notes: Unseeded player from West Germany at the time. Won again in 1986 and 1989.

questions.q: In 1968, tennis entered the 'Open Era,' meaning:
choices: Professional and amateur players competed together

source.notes: Prior to 1968, Grand Slams were amateur-only. The Open Era began at the 1968 French Open.

questions.q: How many Grand Slam singles titles has Serena Williams won (career total)?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: fixed inconsistent original_a metadata (was '12'). 23 Grand Slams. Retired September 2022. One behind Margaret Court's all-time record of 24.

questions.q: Which Spanish player is dubbed the 'King of Clay' for his dominance at the French Open?

source.notes: 14 French Open titles — most at any single Grand Slam. 22 total Grand Slams.

questions.q: In tennis, what is a score of 40-40 called?

source.notes: Next point wins 'advantage'; following point takes the game. Can continue indefinitely.

questions.q: What does 'love' mean in tennis scoring?

source.notes: Etymology debated — possibly from French 'l'oeuf' (egg, meaning zero).

questions.q: Which tennis player has won the most men's Grand Slam titles (24, tied with Margaret Court in women's)?

source.notes: 24 Grand Slams. Serbian. Tied with Margaret Court as the all-time Grand Slams leader (any gender).

questions.q: The Australian Open is traditionally held in which month?

source.notes: Played in Melbourne's height of summer. First Grand Slam of the year.

questions.q: Which stadium hosts the US Open tennis tournament?

source.notes: Named after African-American tennis champion. Part of the Billie Jean King National Tennis Center.

questions.q: In 2008, Rafael Nadal completed the 'Channel Slam' — winning which two titles in the same calendar year, joining Rod Laver and Björn Borg?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: reframed away from 'second man… FO+W+Olympics' (Agassi never did this in one year). Nadal's 2008 Channel Slam (FO+W same year) made him the third man to do it after Laver (1969) and Borg (1978-80).

questions.q: Which annual team event is the oldest international tennis competition?

source.notes: Started 1900. Annual international men's team tennis. Renamed Billie Jean King Cup (women) from Fed Cup in 2020.

questions.q: What year did tennis first become an Olympic sport (before being reintroduced in 1988)?

source.notes: Athens, Greece — first modern Olympics. Dropped after 1924, reintroduced 1988 in Seoul.

questions.q: What is the maximum number of sets in a men's Grand Slam final?

source.notes: Best of 5 sets. Women play best of 3 sets even in Grand Slams.

questions.q: Which tournament is known as the 'fifth Grand Slam,' held annually at Indian Wells, California?

source.notes: Also called 'Tennis Paradise.' Largest tournament outside of Grand Slams.
